creator: antoine@FreeBSD.org
text:
So I did an exp-run with "like GNU strip" added to strip -V

Here are the new failures on i386:

http://package18.nyi.freebsd.org/data/headi386-default/2015-03-19_08h22m45s/logs/errors/deadbeef-0.6.2_3.log
http://package18.nyi.freebsd.org/data/headi386-default/2015-03-19_08h22m45s/logs/errors/gnatdroid-armv7-20141023.log
http://package18.nyi.freebsd.org/data/headi386-default/2015-03-19_08h22m45s/logs/errors/ntop-5.0.1_8.log

Here are the new failures on amd64:

http://package18.nyi.freebsd.org/data/headamd64-default/2015-03-19_16h23m02s/logs/errors/deadbeef-0.6.2_3.log
http://package18.nyi.freebsd.org/data/headamd64-default/2015-03-19_16h23m02s/logs/errors/jpeg-turbo-1.3.1_2.log
http://package18.nyi.freebsd.org/data/headamd64-default/2015-03-19_16h23m02s/logs/errors/libjpeg-turbo-1.3.1_5.log
http://package18.nyi.freebsd.org/data/headamd64-default/2015-03-19_16h23m02s/logs/errors/gnatdroid-armv7-20141023.log
http://package18.nyi.freebsd.org/data/headamd64-default/2015-03-19_16h23m02s/logs/errors/ntop-5.0.1_8.log

The deadbeef and ntop failures look similar (archive inside an archive)
